programme.desc: Aspiring neurosurgeon Howra stitches a patient's skull together after brain surgery, while on the night shift, Sofia's training is put to the test as she verifies a death and deals with a patient who is in severe pain. Nick questions his abilities after failing to catheterise two patients, while second-year junior doctor Luke is also feeling nervous about treating patients independently(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation has called stress the 'health epidemic of the 21st century', and it has been blamed for contributing to illnesses such as diabetes, dementia and types of cancer. Here, Fiona Phillips explores scientific research into stress and demonstrates a number of techniques designed to decrease common factors. She also investigates whether it is inherently a bad thing, joining a number of volunteers who put themselves into a number of high-stress situations to truly understand its meaning and how people could use it to their advantage(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane invites singer-songwriter and YouTube superstar Conor Maynard to join in on a foolhardy trek through the jungles of Costa Rica. The pair must overcome an impenetrable language barrier to communicate with members of the Maleku tribe, who challenge them to reach a destination in the middle of the unforgiving jungle. Equipped with only their smartphones, Russell and Conor must transform their urban devices into effective multi-tools, using backlights to attract grasshoppers for dinner, and navigating the dangerous terrain using a built-in compass. However, deadly bullet ants, river rapids and poor internet connectivity all conspire to thwart their journey back to the safety of their hotel(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King embark on a 2,000-mile journey along the famous American road, taking them through eight different states, each with their own history, flavours and people. They begin by learning about Chicago's meatpacking industry, before moving on to small-town American, where they spend time with the Amish community, cooking together and learning about their different way of life. Their first leg ends in St Louis, where they try 'concrete custard' - a treat so rich and thick it has to be served upside down(n)

programme.desc: This episode looks at life on the highest mountain range on Earth, the Himalayas, home to many extraordinary animals and remarkable ancient cultures. The programme follows snow leopards as they creep into isolated mountain villages searching for food, and monkeys huddling for warmth in a hidden, frozen forest, and the athletes who compete in the gruelling Everest marathon(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer in Scotland has to rescue his own father, whose fishing boat is drifting powerless towards a rocky shoreline. In Bristol, a father and son are among the crew that comes to the aid of two five-year-old girls who have drifted into the Bristol channel on an inflatable, while in Ilfracombe, lifeboat volunteers race against time to save a mother and her daughter stranded at the base of huge sea cliffs(n)

programme.title: Love in the Countryside
programme.desc: Sara Cox delivers a collection of love letters to George, a 24-year-old vet in Hay-on-Wye who has not had a relationship in more than two years. She helps him to arrange a speed dating evening with the writers of his favourite letters - but meeting them face to face proves nerve-racking. Dairy farmer David invites three potential partners to stay with him in Cumbria so they can get a taste of rural life. Plus, an update on Martin and Grace, to see how they are getting on in their dating journeys(n)

programme.title: Defending the Guilty
programme.desc: An unusual series of incidents occurs on the London underground, and Will and Caroline find themselves lumbered with representing a man involved. As they delve deeper into the case, they find him difficult to get a handle on, and struggle to decide if he is just a harmless eccentric, or something much creepier. Will takes the opportunity to get to know his mentor a little better - and soon regrets it. Meanwhile, Ashley begins making some political moves. Legal sitcom, starring Will Sharpe and Katherine Parkinson(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: The final episode examines the infamous Night of the Long Knives in late June and early July of 1934, when Adolf Hitler - urged on by Hermann Goering and Heinrich Himmler - ordered a series of political executions on those seen as a threat to the Nazi regime. The most well-known of these was Ernst Roehm, commander and co-founder of the Sturmabteilung, the Nazi Party's militia, whom Hitler had come to see as a potential rival. At least 85 people died during the purge, although the final death toll may have been in the hundreds(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life

They will be looking to improve on last year's position of fifth, but reigning champions China will start as the favourites. Cameras follow a female puma and her three cubs as they hunt in the mountains of the frozen south, and the descendants of the Inca gather in an ancient ceremony to build a bridge made from woven grass(n)

programme.desc: As the Channel Tunnel's 25th anniversary collides with Brexit, meet the teams behind the scenes and beneath the seabed who keep everything on track through the longest undersea tunnel in the world(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice: You're Fired
programme.desc: The companion programme to The Apprentice, with Tom Allen talking to the second candidate to be ejected from the competition. The host is also joined by a panel of experts and fans, who offer an entertaining analysis of this week's task(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein re-examine a case of murder and mistaken identity from 1900, when a woman's body was found among the sand dunes of Great Yarmouth. A clue from her clothing allowed the police to name her as Mary Jane Bennett, and suspicion immediately landed on her estranged husband, Herbert Bennett(n)

programme.title: Escape from Dubai: The Mystery of the Missing Princess
programme.desc: In February, the 32-year-old daughter of the ruler of Dubai boarded a boat to start a new life in America. But within days Princess Latifa was intercepted by Indian commandos and she was presumably returned to Dubai. This documentary pieces together her life, revealing how she had been planning her escape for more than seven years, and investigates the mystery of her older sister, Shamsa, who disappeared from the streets of Cambridge in 2000 after fleeing the family's British mansion. The film also asks if the image of Dubai being sold to the public, of winter sunshine and luxury hotels, is hiding a brutal dictatorship of human rights abuses(n)

programme.title: The $50m Art Swindle
programme.desc: Documentary telling the remarkable story of a charlatan art dealer who swindled more than $50million from the art establishment before going on the run. Michel Cohen, a popular and charming New York art dealer originally from France, was a high-school drop-out from a poor background who went on to become a rich and successful art dealer. Throughout the 1990s, he sold paintings by artists such as Picasso, Monet and Chagall to America's wealthiest elite. Cohen was living the high life until he began trading recklessly in the stock market and ran up considerable debts. In an attempt to recoup his losses, he swindled private collectors, auction houses and other art dealers out of more than $50million. When his crimes were discovered, he fled the US with his wife and two children and went on the run. In 2003, he was found by Interpol in Brazil and was imprisoned in Rio de Janeiro, but seven months later while awaiting extradition to the US, he escaped from prison and vanished off the face o(n)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Files
programme.desc: As Britain loses its measles-free status and cases surge worldwide, the World Health Organisation has labelled 'vaccine hesitancy' a top 10 threat to global health. But campaigners claim that vaccines are unsafe, and the cause of a health catastrophe. Decades of scientific research do not back them up. Yet their ideas not only persist - they are reaching around the globe. This documentary examines the arguments of those who campaign against vaccines, and hears from people at the heart of alleged conspiracies. And it analyses how vaccine-sceptic ideas are spreading, what gives them currency and who may be benefiting(n)

Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket(n)

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In this episode, Alice Roberts joins archaeologists in the North as they uncover writing tablets, swords and domestic items left by Romans at Vindolanda during a time of British rebellion, before finding traces of a long lost monastery and pilgrimage site on the Scottish island of Iona. Meanwhile in the east of Scotland, a weapons hoard belonging to a wealthy Bronze Age warrior is unearthed(n)

programme.title: Live Gymnastics
programme.desc: Matt Baker presents live coverage of the women's all-around final from Hanns Martin Schleyer Halle in Stuttgart, Germany. All eyes will be on American superstar Simone Biles, who is looking to extend her record after becoming the first woman to win this event four times when returning last year following a lengthy hiatus from competition. With analysis from Beth Tweddle, and commentary by Christine Still and Craig Heap(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: The Channel Tunnel - Life on the Inside
programme.desc: With up to 400 trains a day running on its 62 miles of track, the Channel Tunnel is the busiest railway system in the world. And doing things at the double is key to its success. Two countries built it. There are two terminals. And there is duplication at both ends. This episode reveals how teams of two work on both sides of the Channel to keep tourists and truckers moving. But how will preparations for Brexit affect such a well-oiled system of twinning and duplication, finely tuned over 25 years?(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the fourth leg of their journey, Dave Myers and Si King travels the 400 miles from Amarillo, Texas to Albuquerque in New Mexico. In Amarillo, the pair visit the oldest burger joint in the city, before riding to Palo Duro Canyon to explore a ghost town. Finally arriving in Albuquerque, they join residents to watch a game at the local ballpark and bake a chili chocolate pie to celebrate their cross-country adventure(n)

programme.title: Million Dollar Wedding Planner
programme.desc: The exclusive world of Lelian Chew, who organises nuptials for the super-rich in the Far East, taking on 10 just ceremonies a year at an average cost of $1million each. Bespoke Cartier jewellery, hundreds of thousands of dollars of flowers and multiple designer wedding dresses are nothing out of the ordinary. But Lelian's job also involves navigating the clash between age-old Chinese marriage customs and the desires of the young, often with outlandish solutions - multiple weddings are becoming the norm for the Chinese super-rich(n)

programme.title: The Mash Report
programme.desc: Nish Kumar and a cast of comedy correspondents including Ellie Taylor and Steve N Allen present the satirical and surreal take on the week's news(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. It begins with the hunt to find the killer of two teenage girls in 1980s Leicestershire, which led to an extraordinary forensic breakthrough - DNA fingerprinting(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: Portugal v England (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the friendly at Estadio do Bonfim in Setubal. While the male Portuguese team are the reigning European and Nations League champions, the women's side are yet to achieve the same level of success, having only once qualified for a major championships. England will enter this match as favourites given they sit some 25 places above their opponents in the world rankings, but they did only manage a narrow 2-1 win when the sides last met in the group stage of Euro 2017(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Mandy McAuley investigates the launch of a renewed campaign of loyalist violence in 1985, particularly in the wake of the Anglo-Irish Agreement, which granted the Republic of Ireland political influence in the North. The programme explores how loyalist groups used intelligence leaks from soldiers and police, and uncovers evidence of MI5 agents operating inside the Ulster Defence Association(n)

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Paul Rose visits Ecuador to examine the tale of an 18th-century expedition in which a group of French intellectuals set out to determine the shape of the Earth. The mission was beset by problems from the start as disease, disputes and affairs set the explorers on separate paths and, while great discoveries were made, the team was still to face theft, death and despair(n)

programme.title: The Last Explorers
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Japan to uncover how Victorian Thomas Blake Glover helped rebel samurai clans overthrow the country's rulers in the 1860s and lay the foundations for one of the most powerful economies in the world(n)

programme.title: Majesty and Mortar: Britain's Great Palaces
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank tells the story of 1,000 years of palace building, examining the mystery of why so many have vanished, and the magic of the ones that have survived. In the first edition, he reveals an extraordinary story of buildings, including the Tower of London and Hampton Court Palace, that cemented the monarchs' claim to the throne, including palaces that have been caught up in some of the most dramatic events in history(n)

programme.title: The Art That Made Mexico: Paradise, Power and Prayers
programme.desc: Alinka Echeverria argues that indigenous artists not only projected the elites in Mexico's ancient civilisations but became power-brokers in the struggles for political dominance(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Data
programme.desc: Mathematician Dr Hannah Fry explores the world of data, revealing exactly what it is and how it is captured, stored, shared and made sense of. She tells the story of the engineers of the data age, who brought about a technological and philosophical revolution, and confesses that, for her, the joy of data is all about spotting patterns(n)

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley explores how the history of the American Revolution has been mythologised and manipulated by generations of politicians, writers and protesters(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez sets ex-forger Shaun Greenhalgh the challenge of making an alabaster carving of the kind mass-produced in Catholic England in the centuries before the Reformation(n)

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He begins by looking at the structure's first appearance during Norman rule in 1066, and then at the siege of Kenilworth Castle 200 years later, revealing how it was initially used as an instrument of invasion, but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the crown. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived(n)

programme.title: Van Meegeren: The Forger Who Fooled the Nazis
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon investigates the story of Dutch artist and forger Han van Meegeren, who made millions during the Second World War selling fake Vermeers in Nazi-occupied Holland. Following a trail of evidence across Europe, the art critic pieces together how Van Meegeren fooled the art establishment - and even swindled Hermann Goering, selling him what was then one of the most expensive paintings in the world(n)

programme.title: Stories from the Dark Earth: Meet the Ancestors Revisited
programme.desc: Archaeologist Julian Richards revisits some of his most important digs to discover how science, conservation and new finds have changed people's understanding of ancient history. He concludes his journey at the excavations of two Anglo-Saxon cemeteries, where he explores the mystery of the Anglo-Saxon invasions that began after the fall of the Roman Empire, discovering fresh clues to some of the first pioneers at the burial site of a warrior and his horse(n)

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto charts how the first Venetians inhabited the lagoon in the fifth century AD and created an almost impregnable location, which took the shape of the Venice known today through pioneering engineering work. He reveals the stunning interiors of the Doge's Palace, the Basilica of St Mark and the Ca da Mosto, and uncovers a secret history - beset by violence and political intrigue(n)

programme.title: The Search for the Lost Manuscript: Julian of Norwich
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez tells the story of the first book written in English by a woman, Revelations of Divine Love by Julian of Norwich. The book was written in 1373 and presents an alternative viewpoint on man's relationship with God, which led to it being suppressed for more than 500 years. She travels from Norwich to Cambrai in Northern France, revealing how the book survived and the stories of women who championed it, and consults with academics and theologians about the book's importance(n)

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis charts the stories of Britain's castles and their roles in the country's history, art and literature. He begins by looking at the structure's first appearance during Norman rule in 1066, and then at the siege of Kenilworth Castle 200 years later, revealing how it was initially used as an instrument of invasion, but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the crown. He also visits Tintagel Castle in Cornwall, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived(n)

programme.title: Van Meegeren: The Forger Who Fooled the Nazis
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon investigates the story of Dutch artist and forger Han van Meegeren, who made millions during the Second World War selling fake Vermeers in Nazi-occupied Holland. programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: Handmade in Bolton
programme.desc: Shaun Greenhalgh is set the task of making a Renaissance animal plate of the type invented by Bernard Palissy. The 16th-century French potter used live reptiles and other animals on his ceramic ware, but for Shaun, killing grass snakes in Lancashire is not an option. So how can he source some examples for his moulds? Presented by Janina Ramirez(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist goes on a grand tour of the planet to explain how the Earth's beauty is created by just a handful of forces, beginning by examining the diversity of shapes in the natural world. Off the coast of Canada, Brian explains how massive icebergs that surge down from Greenland and into shipping lanes of the Atlantic emerge due to a powerful yet infinitesimal force of nature, while revealing how honeycombs made by bees in Nepal to store their precious honey conceal a mathematical rule(n)

programme.title: Eugenics: Science's Greatest Scandal
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part documentary exploring the shocking story of eugenics, the controversial idea that the human race can be improved by selective breeding. It is commonly thought that the idea disappeared after the horrors of the Nazi Holocaust, but as science journalist Angela Saini and disability rights activist Adam Pearson discover, practices such as the sterilisation of the poor continued on a global scale for many more years(n)

programme.title: World War Two: 1945 and the Wheelchair President
programme.desc: David Reynolds examines the wartime leadership of US president Franklin Roosevelt, who was burdened by secrets about his failing health and strained marriage(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns what happens to the food we eat and discovers how meringue is made(n)

programme.title: Catie's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Catie drives a supercar, shows us around a really fancy superyacht and then challenges herself to drive a stretch limousine around her track without spilling any drinks(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter uses a model owl to scare off Old Brown, but he ends up scaring the squirrels as well(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Xuli finds a shipwreck on a coral reef, apparently leaking oil. However, as it is raised an octopus swims out squirting ink(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Gardener Will Barrow waters some funny flowers, and The Lost Pirate has an interesting way of paying for his supermarket shopping. Meanwhile, Keith Fitt joins in a skipping class(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie and Doug enjoy their first playdate together with their friends. They try out their new ball pit, but Waffle cannot join in because he's not toilet trained and he has to find fun elsewhere(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack buys Daisy a puppy called Little Mo. Moira says no pets are allowed in the Big Hub, so Molly and Mack try to hide Little Mo, but then she escapes(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion rides out on the Bumparoller to help all of those around him(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Baby Soup Dragon is upset because his mother will not let him use her big ladle to help make the soup, so Tiny and Small tell him he can give them a hand instead(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The friends run around the garden so much that Makka Pakka cannot wash their faces(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben gets a threatening call to say Lola has been kidnapped, and in his panic he insists on Martin helping him, agreeing to let Stacey come home if they get Lola back. Habiba confronts Honey about Adam's behaviour, telling her she deserves better, while Mariam apologises to Iqra and assures her she is proud of her. Rainie sees a glimmer of hope when Max asks her to watch Abi and with Bailey missing, the Taylors rally round to find her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric tries to keep his diagnosis - and the life-or-death decision it has presented him with - a secret from Darla, who is wrestling with a tough choice of her own. Essie is confronted by Isla's angry father, who demands to know the identity of her real mother and threatens to expose the illegal adoption. Kian discovers one of his patients is an old flame, but their unresolved feelings for each other may threaten his judgement(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The mysterious Jessica Mallory arrives in London as the most closely guarded secrets of the intelligence services face exposure. Rachel faces a terrible choice that will not only decide Shaun's fate but also have an impact on the nation's entire criminal justice system. Famke Janssen (X-Men) guest stars in the conclusion of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Aspiring neurosurgeon Howra stitches a patient's skull together after brain surgery, while on the night shift, Sofia's training is put to the test as she verifies a death and deals with a patient who is in severe pain. Nick questions his abilities after failing to catheterise two patients, while second-year junior doctor Luke is also feeling nervous about treating patients independently(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation has called stress the 'health epidemic of the 21st century', and it has been blamed for contributing to illnesses such as diabetes, dementia and types of cancer. Here, Fiona Phillips explores scientific research into stress and demonstrates a number of techniques designed to decrease common factors. She also investigates whether it is inherently a bad thing, joining a number of volunteers who put themselves into a number of high-stress situations to truly understand its meaning and how people could use it to their advantage(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Consumer affairs show tackling issues around the UK. In a battle against suspect deals, the team digs into the facts and demands answers from people at the top, as well as showing viewers how to get the necessary information to make practical choices about their finances(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Rob and Gerald search for a pub landlord who has not paid for his stock, and the owner of a garage risks losing valuable equipment if he does not pay what he owes.(n)

programme.desc: Judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace watch over the four remaining celebrities as they are challenged to present a dish inspired by a place that they love. programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For the second task of the competition, the contestants are challenged to create and sell their own range of ice lollies, pitching one product to a corporate client, and selling another to the general public. In the kitchen, one team decides to opt for risky ingredients, while on the other side, a last-minute switch leads to a tough corporate meeting. After the aspiring apprentices discover who is victorious, one of the losers will be shown the door by Alan Sugar before facing that notorious taxi ride home(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Live
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad and Bethan Rhys Roberts present a weekly show featuring stories and in-depth interviews about what is happening in Wales and why it matters(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby is aiming high and has his sights set on turning over a £1million profit. To hit his target he decides to launch both a 40 per cent-off promo and a key new collaboration both in the same week. This time it's 2018 Love Island winner Dani Dyer's first swimwear collection(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane invites singer-songwriter and YouTube superstar Conor Maynard to join in on a foolhardy trek through the jungles of Costa Rica. The pair must overcome an impenetrable language barrier to communicate with members of the Maleku tribe, who challenge them to reach a destination in the middle of the unforgiving jungle. Equipped with only their smartphones, Russell and Conor must transform their urban devices into effective multi-tools, using backlights to attract grasshoppers for dinner, and navigating the dangerous terrain using a built-in compass. However, deadly bullet ants, river rapids and poor internet connectivity all conspire to thwart their journey back to the safety of their hotel(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Beckenham in Bromley, Greater London, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: The team goes salvaging at Llanfoist Recycling Centre, and transforms an old wooden door and a radiator, as well as making lights from a collection of discarded bottles(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Patrick Kielty present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben gets a threatening call to say Lola has been kidnapped, and in his panic he insists on Martin helping him, agreeing to let Stacey come home if they get Lola back. Habiba confronts Honey about Adam's behaviour, telling her she deserves better, while Mariam apologises to Iqra and assures her she is proud of her. Rainie sees a glimmer of hope when Max asks her to watch Abi and with Bailey missing, the Taylors rally round to find her(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric tries to keep his diagnosis - and the life-or-death decision it has presented him with - a secret from Darla, who is wrestling with a tough choice of her own. Essie is confronted by Isla's angry father, who demands to know the identity of her real mother and threatens to expose the illegal adoption. Kian discovers one of his patients is an old flame, but their unresolved feelings for each other may threaten his judgement(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Mandy McAuley investigates the launch of a renewed campaign of loyalist violence in 1985, particularly in the wake of the Anglo-Irish Agreement, which granted the Republic of Ireland political influence in the North. The programme explores how loyalist groups used intelligence leaks from soldiers and police, and uncovers new evidence of MI5 agents operating inside the Ulster Defence Association(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The mysterious Jessica Mallory arrives in London as the most closely guarded secrets of the intelligence services face exposure. Rachel faces a terrible choice that will not only decide Shaun's fate but also have an impact on the nation's entire criminal justice system. Famke Janssen (X-Men) guest stars in the conclusion of the crime drama(n)

programme.title: Junior Doctors: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Aspiring neurosurgeon Howra stitches a patient's skull together after brain surgery, while on the night shift, Sofia's training is put to the test as she verifies a death and deals with a patient who is in severe pain. Nick questions his abilities after failing to catheterise two patients, while second-year junior doctor Luke is also feeling nervous about treating patients independently(n)

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: The World Health Organisation has called stress the 'health epidemic of the 21st century', and it has been blamed for contributing to illnesses such as diabetes, dementia and types of cancer. Here, Fiona Phillips explores scientific research into stress and demonstrates a number of techniques designed to decrease common factors. She also investigates whether it is inherently a bad thing, joining a number of volunteers who put themselves into a number of high-stress situations to truly understand its meaning and how people could use it to their advantage(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits an former chip shop in Cumbria, Martin Roberts examines a one-bedroom flat in London, and Dion Dublin appraises a plot of land in Greater Manchester. This varied trio of auction lots are all ripe for reinvention, so the presenters pick the minds of their new owners to hear their plans(n)

programme.desc: Met Police officers head to Liverpool to arrest a man wanted in Bermuda, while in Derbyshire, detectives are looking for a Latvian man with a long history of crime(n)

programme.desc: Rob and Gerald search for a pub landlord who has not paid for his stock, and the owner of a garage risks losing valuable equipment if he does not pay what he owes.(n)

After one cook leaves the competition, the final three contestants embark on their penultimate challenge, The Chef's Table, where they are tasked with delivering food of an exceptional standard to impress some of the best chefs in the country(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For the second task of the competition, the contestants are challenged to create and sell their own range of ice lollies, pitching one product to a corporate client, and selling another to the general public. In the kitchen, one team decides to opt for risky ingredients, while on the other side, a last-minute switch leads to a tough corporate meeting. After the aspiring apprentices discover who is victorious, one of the losers will be shown the door by Alan Sugar before facing that notorious taxi ride home(n)

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates with the studio audience and guest panellists(n)

programme.title: Breaking Fashion
programme.desc: Adam Frisby is aiming high and has his sights set on turning over a £1million profit. To hit his target he decides to launch both a 40 per cent-off promo and a key new collaboration both in the same week. This time it's 2018 Love Island winner Dani Dyer's first swimwear collection(n)

programme.title: Stupid Man, Smart Phone
programme.desc: Russell Kane invites singer-songwriter and YouTube superstar Conor Maynard to join in on a foolhardy trek through the jungles of Costa Rica. The pair must overcome an impenetrable language barrier to communicate with members of the Maleku tribe, who challenge them to reach a destination in the middle of the unforgiving jungle. Equipped with only their smartphones, Russell and Conor must transform their urban devices into effective multi-tools, using backlights to attract grasshoppers for dinner, and navigating the dangerous terrain using a built-in compass. However, deadly bullet ants, river rapids and poor internet connectivity all conspire to thwart their journey back to the safety of their hotel(n)

programme.title: The Met: Policing London
programme.desc: In 2018 the highest number of under 25s were killed in London in more a decade. This documentary follows the investigation into two of these murders in the borough of Haringey. DCI Luke Marks picks up the case of 22-year-old youth worker Kobi Nelson, who was stabbed near his home and died at the scene. Less than five weeks later, 19-year-old Kelvin Odunuyi is shot dead in Wood Green. Kelvin was a good student although his mother believes he was being groomed by local gangs. Was he killed in direct retaliation for Kobi's murder? It's up to Luke Marks and his team to find out(n)

programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Aberdeen Gaelic School opened, many in the city are against the language. programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Warwick Davis. Five friends from Surrey team up to answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants who have never met before pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

The quirky design incorporates a circular seating area with a vintage fireplace as a focal point, and uses special planting to bring the area to life(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Japan
programme.desc: Joanna begins a 2,000-mile journey across Japan in Hokkaido, where she meets one of the most important animals in Japanese culture, the red-crowned crane. She arrives in Sapporo during the middle of the annual Snow Festival and meets members of the local indigenous community, before travelling into the Fukushima exclusion zone and taking a bullet train from Nagano to Tokyo(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott head the cast of this action drama, now well into its third and final season. Murtaugh (Wayans) takes a family holiday, but is pulled back into work after witnessing an alleged abduction. Meanwhile, Cole (Scott) is left to investigate a murder by himself. Later, however, the two cases begin to intersect. Then, Erica's mother, Senator Malick comes to town and stirs things up between her daughter, Cole and the case at hand. Kevin Rahm, Johnathan Fernandez and Michelle Mitchenor also star(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken shares Sinead's prognosis with the partygoers, Paula persuades Sophie to follow her heart and Max hides a secret from Shona(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Ruth is concerned about becoming increasingly forgetful, and is even more perturbed when Martin offers to perform a mini mental state examination. It confirms she is showing early signs of dementia. Meanwhile, Louisa plans a special evening to celebrate Martin's birthday, and Al decides to throw Morwenna a surprise engagement party on the same night. There are also problems when Louisa's class tutorial ends in disaster. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with members of Westminster, topical guests and cultural figures. With updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key-players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The host chats to Mel B about her life and career. The singer looks back on her rise to fame with the Spice Girls, as well as sharing revelations about her recent divorce, her struggles with drugs and alcohol, her suicide attempts, and her relationships with fellow Spice Girl Geri Horner and Hollywood star Eddie Murphy(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: JIll Douglas presents action from the latest pool fixtures in Japan - Wales v Fiji at Oita Stadium, Scotland v Russia at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa, and Argentina v USA at Kumagaya Rugby Stadium(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The television presenter and comedian takes on an epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter, a spectacular but deadly time of year to see the unforgiving, ice-bound region. His odyssey is full of surprises as he witnesses natural wonders and discovers the magnetic lure of the far north. The first edition sees Alexander begin in Scandinavia and head toward Iceland, as he swims with locals in the ocean, faces the worst storm in 25 years, and goes down the world's first major tunnel to be built into the heart of a glacier(n)

programme.title: The Chase

Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron attempts to raise Robert's spirits, Moira's guilt continues to weigh on her, and Mandy receives a surprise visitor.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Record numbers of people are now being treated for a whole range of problems from anxiety and depression to critical illnesses like dementia. Mental health problems are as common in older adults as younger ones, yet it is claimed people are less likely to get help as they age. Fiona Foster investigates Britain's mental health crisis(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert and Victoria prepare for the future, Mandy capitalises on an opportunity, and Charity continues to tease Tracy(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.title: British Touring Car Championship Highlights
programme.desc: Steve Rider presents action from round nine at Silverstone, where the 25th, 26th and 27th races of the season were scheduled to take place. With reports by Louise Goodman and commentary from David Addison and Tim Harvey(n)

A series of VIPs travel through Heathrow Airport terminals. Meanwhile, a team of hockey champions try to use their gold medals to get an upgrade(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken shares Sinead's prognosis with the partygoers, Paula persuades Sophie to follow her heart and Max hides a secret from Shona(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Ruth is concerned about becoming increasingly forgetful, and is even more perturbed when Martin offers to perform a mini mental state examination. It confirms she is showing early signs of dementia. Meanwhile, Louisa plans a special evening to celebrate Martin's birthday, and Al decides to throw Morwenna a surprise engagement party on the same night. There are also problems when Louisa's class tutorial ends in disaster. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Eileen Atkins(n)

programme.title: Peston
programme.desc: Political magazine show presented by Robert Peston, featuring major interviews with members of Westminster, topical guests and cultural figures. With updates from social media taking in perspectives from viewers, experts and key-players throughout the programme(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: The host chats to Mel B about her life and career. The singer looks back on her rise to fame with the Spice Girls, as well as sharing revelations about her recent divorce, her struggles with drugs and alcohol, her suicide attempts, and her relationships with fellow Spice Girl Geri Horner and Hollywood star Eddie Murphy(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: JIll Douglas presents action from the latest pool fixtures in Japan - Wales v Fiji at Oita Stadium, Scotland v Russia at Shizuoka Stadium Ecopa, and Argentina v USA at Kumagaya Rugby Stadium(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The television presenter and comedian takes on an epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter, a spectacular but deadly time of year to see the unforgiving, ice-bound region. His odyssey is full of surprises as he witnesses natural wonders and discovers the magnetic lure of the far north. The first edition sees Alexander begin in Scandinavia and head toward Iceland, as he swims with locals in the ocean, faces the worst storm in 25 years, and goes down the world's first major tunnel to be built into the heart of a glacier(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Lenny Henry shares stories from his 40 years in showbusiness, talking about his new UK tour and his autobiography. The comedian also touches on his fight to change the school curriculum and his calls to save free TV licences for over-75s(n)

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly(n)

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom(n)

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news and lifestyle features, including an interview with Sienna Miller about her new movie American Woman(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: The women put the world to rights once more, discussing everything from politics to parenting, and invite a celebrity guest to chat about what they are up to - or anything else that takes their fancy(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team value antiques in Widnes, Cheshire, where James Layte looks at a porcelain figure, Cheryl Hakeney bids on an engagement ring and Chris Skitch is interested in a gramophone(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five players answer questions about top 10 lists. Subjects include rugby union, Grease and boys' names beginning with J. Then they try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Andrew, Arv, Poppy and Issy pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Aaron attempts to raise Robert's spirits, Moira's guilt continues to weigh on her, and Mandy receives a surprise visitor.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Record numbers of people are now being treated for a whole range of problems from anxiety and depression to critical illnesses like dementia. Mental health problems are as common in older adults as younger ones, yet it is claimed people are less likely to get help as they age. Fiona Foster investigates Britain's mental health crisis(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Robert and Victoria prepare for the future, Mandy capitalises on an opportunity, and Charity continues to tease Tracy(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady's Little Heroes
programme.desc: Paul meets 13-year-old Noah, who has checked into the neurology ward ahead of an operation to correct a structural defect of the skull. The youngster needs to overcome his anxiety, however, so Paul joins him on a fun tour aimed at demystifying surgical theatres. Other children include seven-year-old Oliver, a long-term patient who is struggling to walk after being struck down by arthritis, and 17-year-old Rue, who has lost much of her sight and is now using various gadgets to re-learn many everyday tasks(n)

programme.title: Inside Prison: Britain Behind Bars
programme.desc: The documentary lifting the lid on life behind bars focuses on HMP Isle of Wight, a specialist prison housing many sex offenders and paedophiles. Intelligence has been gathered that a USB stick containing child pornography is doing the rounds, so the officers launch a search. But finding such a small item in a vast prison is no easy task. The staff also face a constant battle against drugs, with the inmates finding increasingly sophisticated and creative ways to smuggle them in, from letters sprayed with a spice solution to hollowed-out chess pieces(n)

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: As the Sanditon summer regatta approaches, Sidney wrestles with his feelings towards an old flame and Young Stringer reveals his feelings to Charlotte. With Lady Denham at death's door, the tensions between Edward, Clara and Esther finally come to a head - with surprising consequences. Penultimate episode of Andrew Davies's adaptation of the unfinished final novel by Jane Austen about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change. Starring Rose Williams, Theo James and Anne Reid(n)

programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The withdrawal of a library book on the day of his death is the only clue to a student's murder. Two people who can be linked to the book give each other an alibi, and the police have their work cut out to identify who is lying. The killer eventually confesses and claims self-defence, forcing Phillips to face up to past demons, while Steel finds himself not only prosecuting the offender but also defending the victim. Freema Agyeman and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: For two years, Stephanie Blake has been tormented by a cyberstalker whose e-mails have grown more intimidating over time, but without an actual physical threat against her, there is little the team can do to help. Then, the case turns into a murder investigation, and one of the officers becomes a key witness for the prosecution, but the question arises as to how far he will go to bring the right man to justice. Guest starring Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral)(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Jack and Blakey turn out to be the only entrants in a gardening competition organised by the depot manager's wife. Seventies comedy, starring Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack persuade Inspector Blake to buy Arthur's old motorbike - but he runs into trouble on the test drive. Comedy, starring Reg Varney and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy wonders whether she is desirable, and Robin's lack of response leaves her downhearted. Comedy, starring Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger gets a frosty response when he introduces new team member Clive to the others, while Kev and Mag present Mim with a motorised lawn-mower. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part one of two. While holidaying in Cornwall with his friend Captain Hastings, the sleuth meets a local woman who has seemingly become unaccountably accident-prone - but soon starts to suspect she is actually a victim of foul play. David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield residents are up in arms over the noisy lorries rumbling through the village on their way to Jim Pinder's quarry, and David joins the protest, only to end up spending a night in the cells. Sheep are stolen from Lily Barton's land, and her neighbour promptly becomes the prime suspect - only for her farm to be set alight. Meanwhile, Bellamy strives to fan the flames of passion with Gina. Jean Alexander guest stars(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth is looking forward to Michael coming home. Jack looks on the new farm as a second chance. Carol confronts Lorraine about the chalet incident(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel is touchy when Sangeeta asks questions about Michael. Alan pours his heart out to Caroline. Kim tries to talk to Chris about Frank's behaviour(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: A hungover Alf apologises to Rita for embarrassing her. Deirdre is sick of running around after Tracy. Bernard gets to know Emily better and she tells him all about Ernie(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken and Deirdre spend the whole night talking, and she is upset to hear Tracy is willing to meet Ken but not her. Bernard tells Emily that he is doubting his faith(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A fox-hunt turns sour when a tramp is found dead in the woods, and matters are further complicated by the murder of the chief suspect's father. As DCI Barnaby investigates, he concludes it is only a matter of time before another member of the tight-knit community suffers the same fate. Mystery drama, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, James Bolam and Janet Dale(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield residents are up in arms over the noisy lorries rumbling through the village on their way to Jim Pinder's quarry, and David joins the protest, only to end up spending a night in the cells. Sheep are stolen from Lily Barton's land, and her neighbour promptly becomes the prime suspect - only for her farm to be set alight. Meanwhile, Bellamy strives to fan the flames of passion with Gina. Jean Alexander guest stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Belgian sleuth's Cornish holiday comes to a dramatic conclusion as he unravels the mystery behind the series of disastrous near-misses that placed the life of a beautiful heiress in danger, and as the truth comes to light, a secret engagement is revealed. David Suchet stars, with Philip Jackson and Hugh Fraser(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective reopens an unsolved case when a skeleton is discovered beneath the burned-out remains of a Whitley Bay nightclub. She suspects the bones belong to a notorious criminal who was once a friend of her father and has been missing for more than 20 years. When another murder occurs nearby, she investigates the possibility that a local crime family may be involved with both deaths, while the original owners of the club also have secrets to hide. Brenda Blethyn stars in an adaptation of Ann Cleeves' 2017 Vera Stanhope novel The Seagull(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate when former top-flight footballer Robbie Nichols is murdered in the East End of London, in what appears to be a robbery gone wrong. They question a man seen in the area on the night of the killing, who protests his innocence and points the finger at a known gangster he claims was also at the scene. With the detectives at odds over who to believe, Steel finds it increasingly difficult to prosecute the accused. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: High-court judge Rachel Callaghan (guest star Juliet Stevenson) is critically injured after being shot in the underground car park of her apartment. When it becomes clear a hitman was responsible, the team tries to discover who hired him. As evidence mounts against the accused, the judge's health deteriorates, and Castle is faced with a choice between friendship and respect for a person's right to die. Bill Paterson, Bradley Walsh and Freema Agyeman star(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim is thrilled after his night with Liz, while Colin resigns from the pub after finding out what she did. Tracy packs her bags and leaves home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre is stunned to find Tracy has gone, and Ken is horrified at the thought of her living with a 22-year-old. Amy asks Liz to give Andy her blessing(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth has second thoughts about visiting Michael in prison. Alan returns from his holiday. Kathy tells Jack that Annie is angry with the family about how she has been treated(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is hooked on Robert's new games console. Frank causes a scene in front of a client and throws Joe out, reminding him that he has been sacked. Mark invites Debbie on a date(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A clairvoyant at Whitby Funfair gives a bride-to-be cold feet about her imminent nuptials. Meanwhile, Bellamy is persuaded to cover at the pub, and is almost caught serving drinks after hours, while a new policeman in the village is suspected of being an imposter. Rural drama, starring Ruth Platt, Robert Hands, Mark Jordan and Craig Heaney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector's mother comes to stay, and proves to be a thoroughly unpleasant person. Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Two women vie for the inspector's affections convinced the stars foretell love. Classic Seventies comedy, starring Stephen Lewis and Bob Grant(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo are seeking an additional female flatmate, so Robin has to get in touch with his feminine side. The first episode of the 1970s comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Sally Thomsett(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger joins forces with Mim to tackle a double challenge - the threat of a game being called off due to rain, and a challenge to his leadership from another member of the team. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Easter arrives and everyone prepares for the race, but Rachel, Sangeeta and Jayesh go missing on the fells. Frank causes trouble in the Woolpack when he sees Kim and Joe together(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe plans to sue Frank for unfair dismissal. Jack warns Joe not to interfere in farm business. Kim finds out that Neil Kincaid has been blocking her business dealings(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jenny knows Robert took Mitzi, and Rita confronts Jenny about her lies. Ken urges Tracy not to hurt Deirdre. Phyllis takes advantage of Percy's jealousy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily helps Bernard organise a car boot sale. Jim tells Denise he does not know where he stands with Liz. Ken meets Craig at Tracy's flat(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits his Aunt Alice, who is recovering from an operation at a nursing home, and finds himself in the middle of a mystery. A resident has been found dead and the official verdict is a heart attack, but as rumours of fraud and deception abound, Alice's friend believes the matron and an obnoxious doctor may have conspired to commit murder. Whodunit, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Liz's past catches up with her, and Vernon's dreams of wealth go up in smoke. Meanwhile, Merton receives crime-fighting assistance from an unlikely source - two locals dressed as Batman and Robin, who have spotted criminal activity on a derelict farm(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: John Barnaby arrives in Midsomer and the headmistress of an upmarket girls' boarding school wastes no time in asking him to patrol a forthcoming classic-car show. However, when the local DJ who was set to judge the vehicles is crushed to death, the detective is soon investigating his first murder case since replacing cousin Tom - and as the death toll rises, he finds himself in a perilous situation. Drama, guest starring David Warner and Samantha Bond, with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the detective's old neighbour tells him she has found a gun in her teenage daughter's bedroom, his first instinct is to quietly retrieve the weapon. However, Helen persuades him to do things by the book and leads a firearms unit to take possession of it - but an innocent man is critically injured in the process, leading to an internal investigation that threatens to break up Banks's team(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. On a desperate search for his abducted daughter, Banks puts pressure on Al to lead him to Jaff, without success. However, Al later offers to help - at a price - and the detective contemplates breaking every rule in the book to meet his demands. Meanwhile, the result of Cabbot's internal investigation has led to Morton's immediate suspension. With her career seemingly in the balance, Tracy in danger and Banks's relationship with Annie close to breaking point, how far will he go to keep his family together? Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.desc: The local vicar visits the ward, and even 'lost sheep' Figgis begins to show his spiritual side(n)

programme.desc: A crook flees justice in the guise of a nun, hotly pursued by Simon Templar - who suddenly finds himself having to take a crash-course in the finer points of flying a stolen experimental plane. Drama, guest starring William Gaunt(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: An undercover detective is murdered, and investigations lead Theo to a Mafia boss out to avenge the death of his own sons. Guest starring Daniel J Travanti (Hill Street Blues)(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A formerly famous theatre star is murdered, and the medical examiner resolves to track down the other half of her entertainment double act(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Crooked travel agent Monty Wiseman persuades Arthur to agree to pick up a coffin at Heathrow airport and store it in the lock-up. However when a coroner's autopsy is ordered, Monty stashes it in a disgruntled Terry's bedroom for safekeeping leading to suspicions that there is more to the corpse than meets the eye. Suzi Quatro and Derek Fowlds guest star, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: South Africa v Canada (Kick-off 11.15am). Craig Doyle presents coverage of the Pool B encounter at Kobe Misaki Stadium. With analysis from Maggie Alphonsi, Gareth Thomas and Clive Woodward, and commentary by Martin Gillingham and Alan Quinlan(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo is falsely accused by bribery by a criminal he has been trying to bring to justice, and has to contend with an ambitious district attorney out to make a name for himself(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott head the cast of this action drama, now well into its third and final season. Murtaugh (Wayans) takes a family holiday, but is pulled back into work after witnessing an alleged abduction. Meanwhile, Cole (Scott) is left to investigate a murder by himself. Later, however, the two cases begin to intersect. Then, Erica's mother, Senator Malick comes to town and stirs things up between her daughter, Cole and the case at hand. Kevin Rahm, Johnathan Fernandez and Michelle Mitchenor also star(n)

programme.desc: Cowley and the team are confused by a chain of events that involves lethal narcotics, a series of killings and a drug addict. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.desc: With Haskins in Toronto, his stand-in DCI Quirk (guest star Bill Maynard) becomes obsessed with catching a gang of thieves. programme.title: American Sniper (2014)
programme.desc: A Navy Seal becomes a legend to his colleagues thanks to his skills as a deadly sniper. On his return to the US following four tours of duty in Iraq, he finds living an ordinary life as a husband and father far more difficult to deal with than the demands of warfare. Biopic based on the experiences of real-life Iraq conflict veteran Chris Kyle, directed by Clint Eastwood and starring Bradley Cooper, Sienna Miller and Kyle Gallner(n)

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Dan Jones examines Britain's most iconic canals, beginning with the 152-mile Grand Union Canal, which runs up the spine of the country. Constructed at the height of the Industrial Revolution, the task challenged even the greatest engineering minds of the time, including pioneers such as James Brindley, the man behind Britain's first commercial canal, and William Jessop, the person with whom responsibility lay for the building of the main link from Birmingham to London. Travelling along its route, Dan experiences first-hand the innovations behind its construction, including the Blisworth tunnel(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of the river, and how it has played a pivotal role in London's development as a major centre for international trade. The first episode focuses on the capital's docklands, which at their peak, employed more than 30,000 workers. A boat captain whose family have hauled cargo along the river for six generations recalls his grandfather's unique talents. Plus, a look at the construction of bridges, sewers, and the flood barrier(n)

programme.title: Inside the Tower of London

programme.title: Building Britain's Canals
programme.desc: Writer and historian Dan Jones navigates Britain's longest canal to be built by just one company, the Leeds and Liverpool Canal. Built to link the economically booming counties of Yorkshire and Lancashire, this 19th-century superhighway was experimental for the time, and made Liverpool the busiest port in the world. In Burnley, the cotton capital of the 19th century, Dan visits the world's only steam-powered cotton mill, where enough cloth was woven each year to reach to the moon and back two-and-a-half times(n)

programme.title: The Highland Midwife
programme.desc: Claire wants a local delivery in Belford Hospital rather than have to visit Inverness to be induced, while in the village of Ellon, Vicky has very high blood pressure and a condition that is dangerous for both mother and baby. programme.title: The Science of Sleep: How to Sleep Better
programme.desc: Part two of two. Amir Khan and Gaby Roslin look at the cases of a woman who has restless leg syndrome, and a man who has had chronic insomnia for 20 years. Elsewhere, the sleep deprivation experiment continues, setting up a mini casino to test for risky behaviour, pain resistance and emotional control. Finally, as the subjects reach the final hours of the challenge, the experiment begins to take its toll as the participants' emotions go into overdrive, with one threatening to quit altogether(n)

programme.title: Doing Drugs for Fun?
programme.desc: Four casual cocaine users talk frankly about their drug use, and learn about the impact of their recreational narcotic of choice on their bodies and society. In the final episode, the four casual cocaine users discover the effect Colombia's super-strength is having on users in Britain, from an increase in first-time addicts to a sharp rise in overdose deaths. Will they continue to take cocaine for fun?(n)

As a consequence, she soon finds herself caught up in a devious power game that forces her to question where her true loyalties lie. Thriller, starring Sierra McCormick and Cassidy Gifford(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
